entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The crop species being studied for genetic enhancement of drought resistance.

entities.name: DROUGHT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of peanut genotypes to maintain productivity under water-limited conditions.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION
entities.description: The rate of water loss from the plant, used as a component of the resource capture model.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a plant uses water for biomass production.

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of grain yield to total above-ground biomass.

entities.name: KERNEL YIELD
entities.description: The total weight of kernels produced per plant or area.

entities.name: HYBRIDISATION
entities.description: The process of crossing peanut genotypes to create segregating populations.

entities.name: INDEX SELECTION
entities.description: A selection method based on a weighted index of multiple traits to identify superior genotypes.

entities.name: HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variation in a trait that is attributable to genetic variation.

entities.name: BROAD-SENSE HERITABILITY
entities.description: A measure of heritability that includes all genetic components, including additive and non-additive effects.

entities.name: STANDARD UNIT HERITABILITY
entities.description: A heritability estimate that is independent of the scale of measurement.

entities.name: VARIANCE COMPONENTS
entities.description: A statistical approach used to estimate broad-sense heritability on a family mean basis.

entities.name: CORRELATION AMONG GENERATIONS
entities.description: A method used to estimate standard unit heritability by analyzing correlations between generations.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: The environmental stress condition characterized by lack of water availability.
